setup.welcomeSubtitle: Welcome! Let's set up your PDNS Manager.
setup.step4Subtitle: Your PDNS Manager is ready. You will be redirected to the dashboard in a few seconds...
setup.pdnsHint: PowerDNS servers can be added easily after installation via the admin panel in your browser.
setup.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Profile, system configuration and server management

integrations.totpHelp: After enabling, the login will ask for a code from an authenticator app in addition to your password.
integrations.totpQrHelp: Open your authenticator app (Google/Microsoft Authy, etc.), add a new account and scan the code, or type the secret manually if scanning is not possible.
integrations.totpSecretLabel: If you cannot scan: secret (manual entry)
integrations.panelTokensHelp: For scripts, CI or tools that should not use a browser. You send the long token as a Bearer token – same rights as you have in the panel (zones, records, …).
integrations.apiInfoTitle: What is the API, where is it documented?
integrations.apiInfoP1: The web UI talks to a REST API under the path {{prefix}} on the same host as the app (e.g. https://your-server:5380).
integrations.apiInfoP2: A panel token is just another way to authenticate to that same API: HTTP header Authorization: Bearer <complete token> instead of a login cookie.
integrations.apiInfoP3: A written overview (curl example, token types) is in the repository file docs/PANEL-API.md. If the admin enables it, interactive OpenAPI is available at /docs (DOCS_ENABLED in the server environment).
integrations.apiDocHint: Long-form text: in the project repository, file docs/PANEL-API.md
integrations.webhooksHelp: Let your own HTTPS endpoint receive a JSON notification when records or imports change, so you can trigger automation, Slack, etc.
integrations.webhookInfoBody: The server sends a POST to your URL with a JSON body. You verify authenticity with the shared secret: compute HMAC-SHA256 over the raw request body in UTF-8 and compare with the value after sha256= in the header X-DNS-Manager-Signature. The secret is shown only once when you create the webhook.
integrations.webhookNameHint: Internal label so you can recognise the entry (e.g. “Prod Slack” or “CI hook”).
integrations.webhookUrlHint: Must be https:// (recommended) or http:// – must be reachable from this PDNS Manager server, not only from your laptop.
integrations.webhookEventsHint: * = all events, or a comma separated list, e.g. record, record.created, zone – narrow filters reduce noise. Technical details are in docs/PANEL-API.md (Webhooks).

settings.profileSubtitle: Personal and business data (email, password, address)
settings.phoneHint: At least one digit, max. 25 characters (e.g. +49 123 456789)
settings.apiKeyKeepPlaceholder: ••••••••  (leave empty to keep the existing key)
settings.revealExistingApiKey: Show existing API key (logged in audit log)
settings.myZonesHint: Zones assigned to you (by administrator). Edit under "My zones".
settings.systemTitleHint: Application name (shown top left in menu)
settings.appBaseUrlHint: Used for links in emails (e.g. password reset). Should be the publicly reachable URL (admin only).
settings.allowRegistrationHint: Shows "Register" link on login page; users can sign up.
settings.allowForgotPasswordHint: Shows "Forgot password?" and allows reset via email (SMTP must be configured).
settings.creatorTextHint: Optional line under the footer (e.g. company/creator).
settings.brandingTransparentHint: Note: Transparent and editable - defaults stay until you overwrite them.
settings.languageHint: Interface language (can be changed by any user)
settings.languageWip: Translation work in progress – missing strings fall back to English.
settings.passwordMinLength: The new password must be at least 8 characters long!
settings.smtpSubtitle: Configure the SMTP server for sending emails (e.g. notifications, password reset)
settings.smtpOnlyWhenEnabled: Emails can only be sent when SMTP is enabled
settings.testEmailSubtitle: Send a test email to verify that everything works
settings.updatesPrivateRepo: Your repository is set to Private. Public updates cannot be displayed here. This is good for security!
settings.serversHint: Server configurations are stored in the database, not in the .env file. The PDNS_SERVERS env variable is only imported on first start.
settings.templatesSubtitle: Create templates with nameservers, SOA settings and default DNS records, applied automatically when creating new domains.
settings.templateRecordsHint: These records are created automatically when you create a new domain with this template. Use @ as placeholder for the domain name.
settings.aboutText: PDNS Manager is a self-hosted admin panel for PowerDNS.
settings.writeToServerHint: Zones and changes are written to this server. With shared database, enable only on one server.
settings.phoneTitle: If filled: at least one digit (international formats allowed)

captcha.title: Captcha
captcha.subtitle: Protect login, registration and password reset against bots and brute-force attempts.
captcha.siteKeyHint: Visible in the browser - the captcha widget needs it to render.
captcha.secretKeyHint: Stays on the server. Used to verify tokens against the provider's API.
captcha.testSubtitle: Solve the captcha and verify that site key and secret key match.
captcha.docsTurnstile: Free, privacy-friendly and works without third-party tracking. Create a site for your domain in the Cloudflare dashboard:
captcha.docsHCaptcha: Free tier available, GDPR-friendly. Create a site key in the hCaptcha dashboard:
captcha.docsRecaptcha: Google reCAPTCHA v2 (the classic 'I'm not a robot' checkbox). Note: collects data from visitors. Create a site here:

welcomeMail.title: Welcome email
welcomeMail.subtitle: Send an email to new users right after they register.
welcomeMail.subject: Subject
welcomeMail.subjectHint: Placeholders are allowed - leave empty to use the default for your language.
welcomeMail.bodyHint: Plain text or HTML. Plain text is auto-wrapped into paragraphs. Leave empty to use the default for your language.
welcomeMail.testSubtitle: Sends the welcome mail with your account as sample data.
welcomeMail.testHint: The test uses your username, display name and the configured base URL to fill the placeholders.

acme.title: ACME / Auto-TLS
acme.subtitle: Let certbot create _acme-challenge TXT records automatically.
acme.intro: Generate a scoped API token below and use it with certbot's --manual-auth-hook. The token can only create or delete TXT records named '_acme-challenge.*' in the zones you allow - nothing else.
acme.tokensSubtitle: One token per machine that needs to renew certificates.
acme.empty: No ACME tokens yet. Create one to let certbot manage TXT records.
acme.confirmDelete: Revoke token "{{name}}"? Any certbot setup using it will stop working.
acme.formZonesHint: Pick the zones this token is allowed to write _acme-challenge records into.
acme.noZones: No zones found - configure a PowerDNS server first.
acme.createdWarning: This is the only time the token will be shown. Copy it now and store it safely (e.g. in /etc/dnsmgr.env on your certbot host).
acme.howtoIntro: Quick recipe for a single-cert renewal:
acme.howtoStep1: Save token + this server URL to /etc/dnsmgr.env (chmod 600).
acme.howtoStep2: Drop scripts/certbot-dns-dnsmanager.sh from the repo to /usr/local/bin/ and make it executable.
acme.howtoStep3: Run certbot with --manual-auth-hook and --manual-cleanup-hook pointing to the script (see snippet below).
acme.scriptHint: The hook script lives in the repo at

dashboard.subtitle: Your DNS management panel
dashboard.allServersOnline: All DNS servers are online and reachable.

users.title: User management
users.zonesSelectHint: Pick zones and set read-only or full access. Unselected zones stay hidden.
users.usernamePattern: Only letters, digits, dot, underscore, hyphen

search.title: Search
search.subtitle: Search domains, records and IPs – partial names work (e.g. "example" finds "example.de"). Auto-search from 3 characters.
search.name: Name

zones.title: DNS zones
zones.importContentMissing: Please paste the zone file contents first.
zones.templateHint: Select a template to automatically apply nameservers, SOA settings and DNS records.
zones.typeHintNative: Standard for standalone or database-replicated servers.
zones.soaEditQuestion: How should the serial be incremented on changes?
zones.soaEditDefault: Usually YYYYMMDD01 format – good for most setups.
zones.soaEditEpoch: Unix timestamp as serial – useful for very frequent updates.
zones.templatePreviewTitle: These records will be created automatically:
zones.domainCleanupHint: Tip: you can paste a full URL (e.g. https://www.example.com/login) – it will be reduced to the bare domain. For reverse zones, a CIDR like 192.168.1.0/24 also works.
zones.nameserverListHint: One nameserver per row. Trailing dot is added automatically.
zones.dnssecRegistrarTitle: Important: add the DS record at your registrar
zones.dnssecRegistrarBody: KSK + ZSK (or a CSK) will be generated. You must add the DS record at your registrar. After the zone exists, open Zone details for that domain – the DS lines appear in the yellow “DS records for your registrar” box at the top (not in the “Add record” form).
zones.reverseZoneCreatedAs: Reverse zone: will be created as {{zone}}.
zones.reverseZoneDetected: Reverse zone detected – will be used as is.
zones.invalidDomainName: „{{name}}" is not a valid domain name (e.g. mydomain.com).

zoneDetail.readOnlyZone: You have read-only access to this zone (no records or DNSSEC changes).
zoneDetail.name: Name
zoneDetail.namePreviewLabel: Will be stored in DNS as
zoneDetail.mainDomainHint: @ = zone apex (root) – becomes exactly one name (see preview above), not “zone.zone”. For subdomains enter e.g. www or mail.
zoneDetail.clone: Use as template for new record
zoneDetail.duplicateRecord: A {{type}} record "{{name}}" with value "{{value}}" already exists.
zoneDetail.noRecordsBody: This zone is reachable but does not contain records. Create the first record or check that the zone is synchronized correctly on the PowerDNS server.
zoneDetail.apexWarningTitle: This combination is unusual or forbidden at the apex (@)
zoneDetail.dnssecRegistrarCardTitle: DS records for your domain registrar
zoneDetail.dnssecRegistrarIntro: To complete DNSSEC at the parent (your registrar), you must publish DS records there. They are computed from the keys on this nameserver – you do not type them into “Add record” here.
zoneDetail.dnssecManualRecordHint: The “Add record” form for types like DNSKEY/DS/RRSIG is for advanced manual use only. For normal setups, enable DNSSEC on the zone first – then copy the DS lines from the list below into your registrar’s DNSSEC form.
zoneDetail.dnssecNotActiveTitle: DNSSEC is not enabled for this zone yet
zoneDetail.dnssecNotActiveBody: When DNSSEC is off, there is no DS data to publish. You can enable it now (PowerDNS will create keys and DS digests), or tick “Enable DNSSEC” when creating a new zone.
zoneDetail.dnssecEnableConfirm: Enable DNSSEC for this zone? PowerDNS will create signing keys (KSK/ZSK or CSK depending on server).
zoneDetail.dnssecEnabledOk: DNSSEC has been enabled. Copy the DS record(s) below to your registrar.
zoneDetail.dnssecDisableHint: Disabling removes zone signing and keys in PowerDNS. Also remove the DS record(s) at your registrar, or the domain may not resolve for validating resolvers.
zoneDetail.dnssecDisableConfirm: Disable DNSSEC for this zone? Signing will stop; remove the DS at your registrar afterwards.
zoneDetail.dnssecDisabledOk: DNSSEC has been disabled. Remove the DS record(s) at your registrar if you have not already.
zoneDetail.dnssecEmpty: No DS data returned – check that signing completed on the PowerDNS server.
zoneDetail.dnssecCardShortHint: If you see several DS lines with the same key type and ID, that is normal: it is one key, with different hash algorithms (SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384). Most registrars only need the SHA-256 line (digest type 2). Open the assistant for field-by-field copy.
zoneDetail.dnssecModalWhyTitle: Why do several lines look the same?
zoneDetail.dnssecModalWhyBody: PowerDNS shows one line per digest type (the third number in the DS). Same key ID and key type means the same key. You usually only enter one DS at the registrar: prefer digest type 2 (SHA-256), unless the registrar explicitly asks for another.
zoneDetail.dnssecModalDigestVariants: All DS variants (copy one row into the registrar)
zoneDetail.dnssecModalDnskeyTitle: If the registrar asks for DNSKEY (flags / algorithm / public key)
zoneDetail.dnssecModalNeedEnable: DNSSEC is not active for this zone yet. Enable it first – then DS data will appear here.
zoneDetail.hintSOA:
Order (space-separated): Primary NS · RNAME (SOA email as hostmaster.example.com.) · Serial · Refresh · Retry · Expire · Minimum.
Example: ns1.example.com. hostmaster.example.com. 2025010101 3600 900 1209600 3600
zoneDetail.hintNS:
Nameserver hostname as FQDN (trailing dot recommended).
Example: ns1.provider.net.
zoneDetail.hintA:
IPv4 address only, no port.
Example: 93.184.216.34
zoneDetail.hintALIAS:
Single target hostname (flat alias, provider-specific).
Example: target.example.com.
zoneDetail.hintMX:
Priority (lower = higher preference) and mail server FQDN.
Example: priority 10, server mail.example.com.
zoneDetail.hintTXT:
Any text. SPF/DKIM/DMARC often use TXT.
Example: v=spf1 include:_spf.example.com ~all
zoneDetail.hintSPF:
Same as TXT content (legacy RR type; TXT is preferred for new SPF).
Example: v=spf1 a mx ~all
zoneDetail.hintSRV:
priority · weight · port · target (FQDN).
Example: 10 5 443 sip.example.com.
zoneDetail.hintHTTPS:
RFC 9460: priority, target, SvcParams (e.g. alpn, port).
Example: 0 pool.example.net. alpn=h2
zoneDetail.hintSVCB:
Same family as HTTPS RR: priority, target, optional params.
Example: 0 pool.example.net. port=443
zoneDetail.hintCAA:
flag, tag, quoted value or ";" for issuewild.
Example: 0 issue "letsencrypt.org"
zoneDetail.hintPTR:
Hostname for reverse zone.
Example: mail.example.com.
zoneDetail.hintTLSA:
usage · selector · matching-type · certificate hash (hex).
Example: 3 1 1 a9b8…
zoneDetail.hintLOC:
RFC 1876 format (lat/long/alt).
Short example: 52 22 23.000 N 4 53 32.000 E 10m
zoneDetail.hintNAPTR:
order · preference · flags · service · regexp · replacement
Example: 100 10 "S" "SIP+D2U" "!^.*$!sip:…" _sip._udp.example.com.
zoneDetail.hintDS:
KeyTag · Algorithm · DigestType · Digest(hex).
Example: 12345 8 2 abcd…
zoneDetail.hintDNSKEY:
Flags · Protocol · Algorithm · public key (Base64).
Example: 256 3 8 AwEAA…
zoneDetail.hintNSEC:
Next owner name · type list (NSEC bitmap).
Example: next.example.com. A AAAA RRSIG NSEC
zoneDetail.hintNSEC3:
Algorithm · Flags · Iterations · Salt · Hash · Next hash · types.
Usually copied from your DNSSEC tooling.
zoneDetail.hintNSEC3PARAM:
Algorithm · Flags · Iterations · Salt(hex).
Required for NSEC3 chains.
zoneDetail.hintRRSIG:
Type · Algorithm · Labels · OrigTTL · Expire · Inception · KeyTag · Signer · Signature(Base64).
Usually generated by DNSSEC tools.
zoneDetail.hintOPENPGPKEY:
OpenPGP packet in Base64 (RFC 7929).
Export with gpg --export etc.
zoneDetail.txtTooLong: Long ({{count}} characters) – will be split into 255-character chunks in DNS.
zoneDetail.apexCnameForbidden: CNAME at the apex (zone root) is not allowed by RFC. Use ALIAS instead, or a direct A/AAAA record.
zoneDetail.apexDsForbidden: DS at the apex of your own zone is not allowed – DS records belong in the PARENT zone (at your domain registrar). You can add DNSKEY records here.
zoneDetail.apexDnameWarning: DNAME at the apex is usually wrong – use CNAME for subdomains or ALIAS at the apex.
zoneDetail.apexPtrWarning: PTR at the apex usually only makes sense in reverse zones (in-addr.arpa).
zoneDetail.quickSpfNote: Add the hosts allowed to send mail under "mx" or "include".
zoneDetail.quickDmarcNote: With p=none you start in monitor mode.
zoneDetail.quickDkimNote: The public key is provided by your mail server.
zoneDetail.quickCaaLabel: CAA (only Let’s Encrypt may issue certificates)
zoneDetail.quickTlsaMailNote: usage=3, sel=1, match=1 = DANE-EE / SPKI / SHA-256
zoneDetail.serverReadOnlyBody: Changes will be refused on this server. Switch to a writable server, or enable "Save" for this server in Settings → DNS servers.
zoneDetail.fanoutInfo: Changes will be saved on {{primary}} and replicated to: {{peers}}.

settingsMore.updatesScriptHint: Uses Git (fetch + latest v* release tag, or pull on main), rebuilds the backend image and restarts containers. Requires a .git folder (install.sh or git clone). A plain git pull often does nothing after a tag-based install—that’s what update.sh fixes.
settingsMore.serversManagedHint: All changes are then managed here via the web interface.
settingsMore.noServerHint: Add your first PowerDNS server to get started. You need the URL and API key of your PowerDNS server.
settingsMore.powerDnsApiUrlHint: URL to the PowerDNS webserver (default port: 8081)
settingsMore.newVersionBannerBody: Installed: {{current}} · GitHub has at least {{latest}}. On the server, run ./update.sh in the project folder (not only git pull if you installed from a release tag).
settingsMore.upToDateBody: Installed version {{current}} is the latest on GitHub.
settingsMore.updateSubtitle: Get the latest features from GitHub
settingsMore.updateHowTitle: How do I update the system?
settingsMore.updateSecurityPara: For security reasons (Docker isolation), the application cannot reinstall itself by clicking a button. That would be a security risk.
settingsMore.updateTerminalPara: You can update the system at any time via your server terminal:
settingsMore.updatePathMissingHint: No install path in .env (INSTALL_PATH). Add it next to compose.yaml, then docker compose up -d — or use install.sh/setup.sh, which set it automatically.

templates.nameserversHint: Separated by comma. Note the trailing dot.
templates.createFirstTemplateHint: Create your first template, e.g. "Standard" with your nameservers and commonly used DNS records.
